meta.title: Pipeline Test — Reading the Room
meta.subtitle: Sample content used to verify server delivery.
meta.description: A throwaway 3-card sample published to confirm the content pipeline works end to end. Safe to delete.
meta.author: AI

cards.title: Baseline First
cards.remember: Watch them calm before you read them stressed.
cards.hook: You can't spot a tell until you know their normal.
cards.theySee: Someone who seems to 'just get' people — because you clocked their resting behavior before the pressure hit.
cards.script: Open with small talk and just observe: pace, posture, where their hands rest. That's the baseline you'll measure every later shift against.
cards.counter: When someone studies you early, give them a flat, even baseline — don't reveal range until you choose to.

cards.title: The Pause Test
cards.remember: Ask, then go quiet. Let the gap pull the truth.
cards.hook: People fill silence with what they really think.
cards.theySee: A calm question followed by patience — most can't help but over-explain into the pause.
cards.script: Ask one clean question, then stop talking. Hold the silence three full seconds longer than feels comfortable.
cards.counter: If someone goes quiet on you, don't rush to fill it — mirror the pause and ask your own question back.

cards.title: Cluster, Not Clue
cards.remember: One signal is noise. Three pointing the same way is data.
cards.hook: A single crossed arm means nothing; a pattern means everything.
cards.theySee: Someone hard to fool, because you wait for several signals to agree before you act.
cards.script: Never act on one tell. Wait for a cluster — words, face, and body all leaning the same direction — before you trust the read.
cards.counter: To stay unreadable, break your own clusters: let your words, face, and posture disagree.
